prompt:
Perform the stockroom lead's read-only audit in this sandbox and save the finished handoff as `audit.txt`. Use the genuine `./stockroom` executable for inventory work; its built-in help documents the interface. Do not inspect or edit its `.stockroom` backing files, and do not invent inventory results.

Find the item named “Cold-Chain Gel Packs” at “Medical Stores” and the item named “Tamper-Evident Labels” at “Returns Center”. The first inventory action must be one shell-tool assistant action that launches both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as one batch. Wait for those results before issuing another inventory action. A branch may be retrieved only when its own search returned exactly one stable ID, and that returned ID must drive the retrieval. When both branches resolve, launch both retrievals concurrently as one batch in the next shell-tool assistant action. Similarly named historical entries must remain excluded. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while still completing any independently resolved branch.

This is read-only: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. Do not use any inventory operation other than the required searches and conditional retrievals.

Write exactly three newline-separated sentences to `audit.txt`, with no heading or bullets: one sentence for the Cold-Chain Gel Packs branch, one sentence for the Tamper-Evident Labels branch, then exactly one final sentence comparing their retrieved statuses. A resolved branch sentence must identify its name, location, and retrieved status; an unresolved branch sentence must say it is unresolved without guessing. If either status needed for the comparison is unavailable, say the comparison is unavailable. Add no recommendation or extra commentary. Run `python3 protected_tests/verify.py` to verify the completed audit.
